        code: \\\"custom\\\",\\n              message: `No duplicates allowed.`,\\n              input: val,\\n            });\\n          }\\n        });\\n    \\n    \\n    ```\\n  </Tab>\\n  <Tab value=\\\"Zod Mini\\\">\\n```ts\\n        const UniqueStringArray = z.array(z.string()).check(\\n          z.superRefine((val, ctx) => {\\n            if (val.length > 3) {\\n              ctx.addIssue({\\n                code: \\\"too_big\\\",\\n                maximum: 3,\\n                origin: \\\"array\\\",\\n                inclusive: true,\\n                message: \\\"Too many items 😡\\\",\\n                input: val,\\n              });\\n            }\\n    \\n            if (val.length !== new Set(val).size) {\\n              ctx.addIssue({\\n                code: \\\"custom\\\",\\n                message: `No duplicates allowed.`,\\n                input: val,\\n              });\\n            }\\n          })\\n        );\\n    ```\\n  </Tab>\\n</Tabs>\\n### `.check()`\\n<Callout>\\n  **Note** — The `.check()` API is a more low-level API that's generally more complex than `.superRefine()`. It can be faster in performance-sensitive code paths, but it's also more verbose.\\n</Callout>\\n<Accordions>\\n  <Accordion title=\\\"View example\\\">\\n    The `.refine()` API is syntactic sugar atop a more versatile (and verbose) API called `.check()`. You can use this API to create multiple issues in a single refinement or have full control of the generated issue objects.\\n    <Tabs groupId=\\\"lib\\\" items={[\\\"Zod\\\", \\\"Zod Mini\\\"]}>\\n      <Tab value=\\\"Zod\\\">\\n```ts\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"Zod v4 llms-full.txt\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"site\",\"url\":\"https://zod.dev/llms-full.txt\"},{\"type\":\"document\",\"source\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"## Error customization\\nZod 4 standardizes the APIs for error customization under a single, unified `error` param. Previously Zod's error customization APIs were fragmented and inconsistent. This is cleaned up in Zod 4.\\n### deprecates `message` parameter\\nReplaces `message` param with `error`. The old `message` parameter is still supported but deprecated.\\n<Tabs groupId=\\\"error-message\\\" items={[\\\"Zod 4\\\", \\\"Zod 3\\\"]} persist>\\n  <Tab value=\\\"Zod 4\\\">\\n```ts\\n        z.string().min(5, { error: \\\"Too short.\\\" });\\n    ```\\n  </Tab>\\n  <Tab value=\\\"Zod 3\\\">\\n```ts\\n        z.string().min(5, { message: \\\"Too short.\\\" });\\n    ```\\n  </Tab>\\n</Tabs>\\n### drops `invalid_type_error` and `required_error`\\nThe `invalid_type_error` / `required_error` params have been dropped. These were hastily added years ago as a way to customize errors that was less verbose than `errorMap`. They came with all sorts of footguns (they can't be used in conjunction with `errorMap`) and do not align with Zod's actual issue codes (there is no `required` issue code).\\nThese can now be cleanly represented with the new `error` parameter.\\n<Tabs groupId=\\\"error-type\\\" items={[\\\"Zod 4\\\", \\\"Zod 3\\\"]} persist>\\n  <Tab value=\\\"Zod 4\\\">\\n```ts\\n        z.string({ \\n          error: (issue) => issue.input === undefined \\n            ? \\\"This field is required\\\" \\n            : \\\"Not a string\\\" \\n        });\\n    ```\\n  </Tab>\\n  <Tab value=\\\"Zod 3\\\">\\n```ts\\n        z.string({ \\n          required_error: \\\"This field is required\\\",\\n          invalid_type_error: \\\"Not a string\\\", \\n        });\\n    ```\\n  </Tab>\\n</Tabs>\\n### drops `errorMap`\\nThis is renamed to `error`.\\nError maps can also now return a plain `string` (instead of `{message: string}`). They can also return `undefined`, which tells Zod to yield control to the next error map in the chain.\\n<Tabs groupId=\\\"error-map\\\" items={[\\\"Zod 4\\\", \\\"Zod 3\\\"]} persist>\\n  <Tab value=\\\"Zod 4\\\">\\n```ts\\n        z.string().min(5, {\\n          error: (issue) => {\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"Zod v4 llms-full.txt\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"site\",\"url\":\"https://zod.dev/llms-full.txt\"},{\"type\":\"document\",\"source\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Title: Zod v4 Should Support Field-Level Cross Validation Without Workarounds\\nCategory: Ideas\\nAuthor: @BittuChan\\nTimestamp: March 15, 2026\\nBody:\\n The Problem\\r\\n\\r\\n  One of the most common validation patterns in real-world apps is cross-field validation — things like \\\"confirm password must match password\\\" or \\\"end date must be\\r\\n  after start date.\\\" In Zod v3, ctx.parent made this possible inside .superRefine(), but in v4 it was removed without a direct replacement.\\r\\n\\r\\n  This has left a gap that's hitting a lot of developers, especially those using form libraries like React Hook Form, where field-level validation is the standard\\r\\n  approach.\\r\\n\\r\\n  Currently the workarounds are:\\r\\n  - Moving validation to the parent object level — but this breaks form-per-field error mapping\\r\\n  - Duplicating logic outside Zod — defeats the purpose of a single source of truth for validation\\r\\n  - Using .pipe() chains — adds complexity for something that should be straightforward\\r\\n\\r\\n  What I'd Like to See\\r\\n\\r\\n  A first-class API for accessing sibling field values during field-level refinement. Something like:\\r\\n\\r\\n  const schema = z.object({\\r\\n    password: z.string().min(8),\\r\\n    confirmPassword: z.string().refine(\\r\\n      (val, ctx) => val === ctx.sibling(\\\"password\\\"),\\r\\n      { message: \\\"Passwords don't match\\\" }\\r\\n    ),\\r\\n  });\\r\\n\\r\\n  Or alternatively, a dependsOn modifier:\\r\\n\\r\\n  const schema = z.object({\\r\\n    startDate: z.date(),\\r\\n    endDate: z.date().dependsOn(\\\"startDate\\\", (end, start) => end > start, {\\r\\n      message: \\\"End date must be after start date\\\",\\r\\n    }),\\r\\n  });\\r\\n\\r\\n  Why This Matters\\r\\n\\r\\n  - Password confirmation, date ranges, conditional required fields — these aren't edge cases, they're everyday patterns\\r\\n  - Form libraries map errors by field path — parent-level refinements break that mapping\\r\\n  - Zod positions itself as the single source of truth for validation and types — cross-field validation is a core part of that story\\r\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"  - Multiple open issues (#5706, #5707) show this is a real pain point across the community\\r\\n\\nComments:\\n\\n    Comment #1 from @manimovassagh on March 20, 2026:\\n\\n    The removal of `ctx.parent` in v4 definitely left a gap here. The cleanest approach right now is `.superRefine()` at the object level with `path` to attach errors to the correct field:\\n\\n```ts\\nconst signupSchema = z\\n  .object({\\n    password: z.string().min(8),\\n    confirmPassword: z.string(),\\n  })\\n  .superRefine((data, ctx) => {\\n    if (data.password !== data.confirmPassword) {\\n      ctx.addIssue({\\n        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,\\n        message: \\\"Passwords must match\\\",\\n        path: [\\\"confirmPassword\\\"],\\n      });\\n    }\\n  });\\n```\\n\\nWith React Hook Form + `@hookform/resolvers/zod`, this maps correctly to field-level errors:\\n\\n```tsx\\nconst { formState: { errors } } = useForm({ resolver: zodResolver(signupSchema) });\\n// errors.confirmPassword.message => \\\"Passwords must match\\\"\\n```\\n\\nThe key insight is `path: [\\\"confirmPassword\\\"]` — RHF resolves errors by path, so even though the refinement lives on the parent, the error shows up on the right field.\\n\\nSame pattern for date ranges:\\n\\n```ts\\nconst dateRangeSchema = z\\n  .object({\\n    startDate: z.coerce.date(),\\n    endDate: z.coerce.date(),\\n  })\\n  .superRefine((data, ctx) => {\\n    if (data.endDate <= data.startDate) {\\n      ctx.addIssue({\\n        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,\\n        message: \\\"End date must be after start date\\\",\\n        path: [\\\"endDate\\\"],\\n      });\\n    }\\n  });\\n```\\n\\nAgreed that a first-class `.dependsOn()` or `ctx.sibling()` API would be better DX long term. But `.superRefine()` with explicit `path` does work well today and integrates cleanly with form libraries.\\n\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"Zod v4 Should Support Field-Level Cross Validation Without Workarounds\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"github_discussion\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/discussions/5780\"},{\"type\":\"document\",\"source\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Github Issue by @PinkHonkKongGmr created on February 11, 2026. The current state of the issue is: open\\n\\nIn **Zod 3**, it was possible to implement reactive cross-field validation for password confirmation using `field.superRefine`. \\n\\nExample (Zod 3):\\n\\n  .superRefine((data, ctx) => {\\n            if (data.confirm !== data.password) {\\n              ctx.addIssue({\\n                code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,\\n                message: t('validation.passwordsMatch'),\\n                path: ['confirm'],\\n              });\\n            }\\n\\n\\nThis worked as expected with React Hook Form and mode: \\\"onChange\\\":\\n\\nThe confirm field would show a password mismatch error reactively as the user typed\\n\\nNo submission was required to trigger the error\\n\\nNote: This reactive feedback occurred only on the confirm field, which is the expected and natural behavior.\\n\\nProblem in Zod 4\\n\\nIn Zod 4, ctx.parent was removed. The recommended approach is now to use object.refine() with a path to target the error:\\n\\nconst schema = z\\n  .object({\\n    password: z.string().min(8),\\n    confirmPassword: z.string(),\\n  })\\n  .refine((data) => data.password === data.confirmPassword, {\\n    message: \\\"Passwords must match\\\",\\n    path: [\\\"confirmPassword\\\"],\\n  });\\n\\nHowever, when using React Hook Form with mode: \\\"onChange\\\":\\n\\nThe confirm error does not appear while typing\\n\\nNo mismatch feedback is shown until the form is submitted\\n\\nAfter submit, reactive updates work, but the initial typing gives no error\\n\\nThis is a regression compared to Zod 3.\\n\\nExpected Behavior\\n\\nCross-field validation for confirm should trigger reactively as the user types\\n\\nPassword mismatch errors should appear without submitting the form\\n\\nBehavior should match Zod 3 superRefine experience\\n\\nEnvironment\\n\\nZod 4.x\\n\\nReact Hook Form 7.x\\n\\nReact 19.x\\n\\nNotes / Reproduction\\n\\nThis issue occurs even when using:\\n\\nuseForm({\\n  resolver: zodResolver(schema),\\n  mode: \\\"onChange\\\",\\n})\\n\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\nIt seems to be a limitation in the interaction between Zod 4's object-level .refine() and React Hook Form's partial validation strategy.\\n\\nA minimal reproduction in CodeSandbox would make this behavior easy to verify.\\n---\\nComment #1 by @pullfrog[bot]:\\n\\nThanks for the report. I've investigated this issue and found several inaccuracies:\\n\\n## Key Findings\\n\\n1. **`ctx.parent` never existed in Zod 3**: The `RefinementCtx` interface in Zod 3 only had `addIssue` and `path` properties. There was no `parent` property.\\n\\n2. **`superRefine` still exists in Zod 4**: The method is fully available and works the same way as in Zod 3. You can continue using `superRefine` for cross-field validation:\\n\\n```typescript\\nconst schema = z.object({\\n  password: z.string().min(8),\\n  confirmPassword: z.string(),\\n}).superRefine((data, ctx) => {\\n  if (data.password !== data.confirmPassword) {\\n    ctx.addIssue({\\n      code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,\\n      message: \\\"Passwords must match\\\",\\n      path: ['confirmPassword'],\\n    });\\n  }\\n});\\n```\\n\\n3. **This is a React Hook Form integration issue, not a Zod issue**: The reactive validation behavior you're experiencing is controlled by React Hook Form's validation strategy, not by Zod. When using `mode: \\\"onChange\\\"`, React Hook Form only re-validates fields that have been touched or have changed. For cross-field validation:\\n   - The `confirmPassword` field error won't appear until either field has been interacted with\\n   - Once validation has run once (e.g., after submit), reactive updates work as expected\\n   - This is React Hook Form's design for performance optimization\\n\\n## Recommendations\\n\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\n- Continue using `superRefine` for cross-field validation (it's the recommended approach)\\n- Consider using `mode: \\\"all\\\"` in React Hook Form if you need more aggressive validation\\n- Use React Hook Form's `trigger()` method to manually trigger validation on related fields\\n- Check React Hook Form's documentation on cross-field validation patterns\\n\\nThis issue doesn't represent a regression in Zod. I'm closing this as the behavior is working as designed, and the reactive validation timing is controlled by React Hook Form's integration layer.\\n<!-- PULLFROG_DIVIDER_DO_NOT_REMOVE_PLZ -->\\n<sup><a href=\\\"https://pullfrog.com\\\"><picture><source media=\\\"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)\\\" srcset=\\\"https://pullfrog.com/logos/frog-white-full-128px.png\\\"><img src=\\\"https://pullfrog.com/logos/frog-green-full-128px.png\\\" width=\\\"9px\\\" height=\\\"9px\\\" style=\\\"vertical-align: middle; \\\" alt=\\\"Pullfrog\\\"></picture></a>  ｜ Triggered by [Pullfrog](https://pullfrog.com) ｜ Using [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) ｜ [View workflow run](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/actions/runs/21906190541) ｜ [pullfrog.com](https://pullfrog.com) ｜ [𝕏](https://x.com/pullfrogai)</sup>\\n---\\nComment #2 by @PinkHonkKongGmr:\\n\\nThanks for the clarification. I understand that `superRefine` works as designed in Zod 4.  \\n\\nHowever, in practice, using `superRefine` with React Hook Form—even with `mode: \\\"onChange\\\"` or `mode: \\\"all\\\"`—**does not trigger reactive validation on the confirm field before submission**.\\n\\nA working approach is to use `object.refine()` with the `when` parameter:\\n\\n```ts\\nconst schema = z.object({\\n  password: z.string().min(8),\\n  confirm: z.string(),\\n})\\n.refine(\\n  (data) => data.password === data.confirm,\\n  {\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\n    message: t('validation.passwordsMatch'),\\n    path: ['confirm'],\\n    when() { return true; },\\n  }\\n);\\n\\n\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"Regression: Confirm field cross-validation (password match) no longer reactive in Zod 4 + RHF\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"github_issue\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5707\"},{\"type\":\"document\",\"source\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Github Issue by @nurbolnygmanov created on September 03, 2025. The current state of the issue is: open\\n\\nI have some complex form validation where I use superrefine as final check for some non related stuff, so problem is when one of base object fields fail, it prevents superrefine form being triggered. Looks like there is a solution for refine with `where` method. But is there smth similar in zod? \\n---\\nComment #1 by @inkeep-ai-assistant[bot]:\\n\\n\\n\\nIn Zod (v4), the default behavior is that refinements (including those added by `.superRefine()` or `.check()`) are considered continuable, meaning Zod will run them even if other validations fail. However, you can control when your custom refinement runs by using the `when` parameter in your refinement definition. This allows you to replicate the \\\"run this only if other fields are valid\\\" logic[(1)](https://zod.dev/api#refinements)[(2)](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/3753).\\n\\nHere's how you can use the `when` parameter to control execution:\\n\\n```typescript\\nconst schema = z\\n  .object({\\n    password: z.string().min(8),\\n    confirmPassword: z.string(),\\n    anotherField: z.string(),\\n  })\\n  .check(z.refine((data) => data.password === data.confirmPassword, {\\n    message: \\\"Passwords do not match\\\",\\n    path: [\\\"confirmPassword\\\"],\\n    when(payload) { \\n      // Only run when there are no issues with these fields\\n      return payload.issues.every((iss) => { \\n        const firstPathEl = iss.path?.[0]; \\n        return firstPathEl !== \\\"password\\\" && firstPathEl !== \\\"confirmPassword\\\"; \\n      }); \\n    },  \\n  }));\\n```\\n[(1)](https://zod.dev/api#refinements)\\n\\nThis ensures your cross-field refinement only runs if the relevant fields are valid. For more details, see the [official Zod docs](https://zod.dev/api#refinements)[(1)](https://zod.dev/api#refinements).\\n\\n📚 **Sources:**\\n1. [Defining schemas](https://zod.dev/api)\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\n2. [Although `z.string().date()` check fails, yet the callback to `.superRefine()` is executed.](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/3753)\\n\\n---\\n\\nHave another question? Just tag [@inkeep](https://inkeep.com).\\n---\\nComment #2 by @nurbolnygmanov:\\n\\n@colinhacks Are there any plans to make superrefine work instantly with other validations?\\nIn real project I have complex validation and I can not use refine with when, it has to be superrefine.\\nHere is my codesandbox link with super minimalistic example.\\nhttps://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/rd7vwt?file=%2Fsrc%2Fdemo.tsx\\n---\\nComment #3 by @annanShao:\\n\\nI have the same problems,it will not trigger superRefine's func before the previous normal check passed\\n---\\nComment #4 by @Siqalexx:\\n\\nHi, did you manage to find a solution to this problem?\\n\\n> I have some complex form validation where I use superrefine as final check for some non related stuff, so problem is when one of base object fields fail, it prevents superrefine form being triggered. Looks like there is a solution for refine with `where` method. But is there smth similar in zod?\\n\\n\\n---\\nComment #5 by @Tasin5541:\\n\\nFacing issues with dependent form field validations because of this. Would be great to have a way to run all the validations together.\\n---\\nComment #6 by @Abdullah-Sajjad026:\\n\\nDoes anybody find a good solution to this problem?\\n\\n---\\nComment #7 by @Tasin5541:\\n\\n> Does anybody find a good solution to this problem?\\n\\nUse multiple chained `refine` with `when() => true` or any other condition you want to add. \\n---\\nComment #8 by @polkolins:\\n\\n@inkeep you said \\n\\n> In Zod (v4), the default behavior is that refinements (including those added by .superRefine() or .check()) are considered continuable, meaning Zod will run them even if other validations fail\\n\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\nBut this is not true actually. `superRefine()` runs only **after** basic schema is valid. If my system has some limits so I can't really use `discriminatedUnion` what would be a solution for schemas like this:\\n\\n```\\nconst baseSchema = z.object({\\n  a: z.number(),\\n  b: z.boolean(),\\n  array: z.array(\\n    z.object({\\n      requiredField: z.number(),\\n      optionalField: z.number().optional(),\\n    })\\n  );\\n});\\n\\nconst finalSchema = baseSchema.superRefine((val, ctx) => {\\n if (val.b) {\\n    val.array.forEach((el, index) => {\\n      if (roof.optionalField === undefined) {\\n          ctx.addIssue({\\n            code: \\\"custom\\\",\\n            path: [\\\"array\\\", index, \\\"optionalField\\\"],\\n            message: \\\"optionalField is required for Rafter roof.\\\",\\n          });\\n        }\\n    });\\n  }\\n})\\n```\\n\\nCan I call `.refine()` here for each element of the array?\\n---\\nComment #9 by @inkeep-ai-assistant[bot]:\\n\\nYes—use `.refine()` on the *array element schema* (or on the array schema) rather than trying to “call refine inside superRefine”. In Zod, `.refine()` is something you attach to a schema; you don’t imperatively invoke it per element during parsing. [(1)](https://zod.dev/api#refinements)\\n\\nAlso, Zod gives you control over *when* a refinement runs via the `when` parameter on `.refine()`. That’s the mechanism intended for “run even if other fields have errors / only depend on a subset of fields”. [(1)](https://zod.dev/api#refinements)\\n\\nHere’s a pattern that matches your case (make `optionalField` required for each array element when `b === true`), while still producing per-element paths like `[\\\"array\\\", index, \\\"optionalField\\\"]`:\\n\\n```ts\\nconst baseSchema = z.object({\\n  a: z.number(),\\n  b: z.boolean(),\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\n  array: z.array(\\n    z.object({\\n      requiredField: z.number(),\\n      optionalField: z.number().optional(),\\n    })\\n  ),\\n});\\n\\nconst finalSchema = baseSchema.refine((val) => {\\n  if (!val.b) return true;\\n  return val.array.every((el) => el.optionalField !== undefined);\\n}, {\\n  message: \\\"optionalField is required when b is true\\\",\\n  path: [\\\"array\\\"],\\n  when(payload) {\\n    // Run this refinement as long as `b` and `array` are valid enough to inspect\\n    return baseSchema\\n      .pick({ b: true, array: true })\\n      .safeParse(payload.value).success;\\n  },\\n});\\n```\\n[(1)](https://zod.dev/api#refinements)\\n\\nIf you need *one issue per failing element* (with the exact `[\\\"array\\\", index, \\\"optionalField\\\"]` path), that’s exactly what `.superRefine()` is for—but the docs in scope here only describe `when` on `.refine()` (not on `.superRefine()`), so the only documented lever I can point to for “run timing” is `when` on `.refine()`. [(1)](https://zod.dev/api#refinements)\\n\\n📚 **Sources:**\\n1. [Defining schemas](https://zod.dev/api)\\n\\n\\n\\n---\\n\\nHave another question? Just tag [@inkeep](https://inkeep.com).\\n\\n\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"[V3-V4] Superrefine is not triggered if object validation fails\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"github_issue\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5197\"},{\"type\":\"document\",\"source\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Title: Zod v4 Should Support Field-Level Cross Validation Without Workarounds\\nCategory: Q&A\\nAuthor: @BittuChan\\nTimestamp: March 15, 2026\\nBody:\\nThe Problem\\r\\n\\r\\nOne of the most common validation patterns in real-world apps is cross-field validation — things like \\\"confirm password must match password\\\" or \\\"end date must be\\r\\nafter start date.\\\" In Zod v3, ctx.parent made this possible inside .superRefine(), but in v4 it was removed without a direct replacement.\\r\\n\\r\\nThis has left a gap that's hitting a lot of developers, especially those using form libraries like React Hook Form, where field-level validation is the standard\\r\\napproach.\\r\\n\\r\\nCurrently the workarounds are:\\r\\n\\r\\nMoving validation to the parent object level — but this breaks form-per-field error mapping\\r\\nDuplicating logic outside Zod — defeats the purpose of a single source of truth for validation\\r\\nUsing .pipe() chains — adds complexity for something that should be straightforward\\r\\nWhat I'd Like to See\\r\\n\\r\\nA first-class API for accessing sibling field values during field-level refinement. Something like:\\r\\n\\r\\nconst schema = z.object({\\r\\npassword: z.string().min(8),\\r\\nconfirmPassword: z.string().refine(\\r\\n(val, ctx) => val === ctx.sibling(\\\"password\\\"),\\r\\n{ message: \\\"Passwords don't match\\\" }\\r\\n),\\r\\n});\\r\\n\\r\\nOr alternatively, a dependsOn modifier:\\r\\n\\r\\nconst schema = z.object({\\r\\nstartDate: z.date(),\\r\\nendDate: z.date().dependsOn(\\\"startDate\\\", (end, start) => end > start, {\\r\\nmessage: \\\"End date must be after start date\\\",\\r\\n}),\\r\\n});\\r\\n\\r\\nWhy This Matters\\r\\n\\r\\nPassword confirmation, date ranges, conditional required fields — these aren't edge cases, they're everyday patterns\\r\\nForm libraries map errors by field path — parent-level refinements break that mapping\\r\\nZod positions itself as the single source of truth for validation and types — cross-field validation is a core part of that story\\r\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Multiple open issues (https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5706, https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/5707) show this is a real pain point across the community\\nComments:\\n\\n    Comment #1 from @Shiven0504 on March 15, 2026:\\n\\n    Hii @BittuChan, thanks for writing it up so clearly. I ran into this exact problem migrating a Next.js app from v3 to v4. I had cross-field validation everywhere password confirmations, date range pickers, conditional required fields based on a toggle and all of it broke overnight when ctx.parent was removed.\\r\\n\\r\\n  What I tried (and why each fell short):\\r\\n  - Object-level .superRefine() -  Works for validation logic, but React Hook Form maps errors by field path. When the error lives on the parent object, the individual field doesn't show it. We had to write custom error-mapping glue code that felt wrong.\\r\\n  - Splitting validation into Zod + manual checks - Ended up with validation logic in two places. Bugs crept in because the Zod schema said the form was valid but our manual check disagreed.\\r\\n  - Using .transform() to sneak in validation - Technically works but it's a hack. Transforms are for data shaping, not validation, and it makes the schema harder to read.\\r\\n\\r\\n Why I think the dependsOn API is the right direction:\\r\\n  The dependsOn approach from the proposal is interesting because it makes the dependency between fields explicit and declarative. The validation engine could use that information to:\\r\\n  - Run dependent validations only after the dependency field passes\\r\\n  - Produce field-level errors that form libraries can map correctly\\r\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"  - Keep the schema readable so that anyone looking at it immediately understands the relationship\\r\\n\\r\\n  That said, I'd add one thing to the proposal which is support for depending on multiple fields:\\r\\n\\r\\n  const schema = z.object({\\r\\n    role: z.enum([\\\"admin\\\", \\\"user\\\"]),\\r\\n    department: z.string(),\\r\\n    accessLevel: z.number().dependsOn(\\r\\n      [\\\"role\\\", \\\"department\\\"],\\r\\n      (level, role, dept) => {\\r\\n        if (role === \\\"user\\\" && dept !== \\\"engineering\\\") return level <= 3;\\r\\n        return true;\\r\\n      },\\r\\n      { message: \\\"Access level too high for this role/department combination\\\" }\\r\\n    ),\\r\\n  });\\n\\n    Replies to comment #1:\\n\\n        Reply #1 to comment #1 from Shiven0504 on March 15, 2026:\\n\\n        thanks for the insight! will adhere to that shortly..\\n\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"Zod v4 Should Support Field-Level Cross Validation Without Workarounds\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"github_discussion\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/discussions/5781\"},{\"type\":\"document\",\"source\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"https://zod.dev/api#template-literals\\n\\n## [Template literals](https://zod.dev/api?id=template-literals)\\n\\n**New** — Introduced in `zod@4.0` .\\n\\nTo define a template literal schema:\\n\\n```\\nconst schema = z.templateLiteral([ \\\"hello, \\\", z.string(), \\\"!\\\" ]);\\n// `hello, ${string}!`\\n```\\n\\nThe `z.templateLiteral` API can handle any number of string literals (e.g. `\\\"hello\\\"` ) and schemas. Any schema with an inferred type that's assignable to `string | number | bigint | boolean | null | undefined` can be passed.\\n\\n```\\nz.templateLiteral([ \\\"hi there\\\" ]);\\n// `hi there`\\n \\nz.templateLiteral([ \\\"email: \\\", z.string() ]);\\n// `email: ${string}`\\n \\nz.templateLiteral([ \\\"high\\\", z.literal(5) ]);\\n// `high5`\\n \\nz.templateLiteral([ z.nullable(z.literal(\\\"grassy\\\")) ]);\\n// `grassy` | `null`\\n \\nz.templateLiteral([ z.number(), z.enum([\\\"px\\\", \\\"em\\\", \\\"rem\\\"]) ]);\\n// `${number}px` | `${number}em` | `${number}rem`\\n```\\n\\n\\n\\nhttps://zod.dev/api#numbers\\n\\n## [Numbers](https://zod.dev/api?id=numbers)\\n\\nUse `z.number()` to validate numbers. It allows any finite number.\\n\\n```\\nconst schema = z.number();\\n \\nschema.parse(3.14);      // ✅\\nschema.parse(NaN);       // ❌\\nschema.parse(Infinity);  // ❌\\n```\\n\\nZod implements a handful of number-specific validations:\\n\\nZod Zod Mini     \\n```\\nz.number().check(z.gt(5));\\nz.number().check(z.gte(5));            // alias .minimum(5)\\nz.number().check(z.lt(5));\\nz.number().check(z.lte(5));            // alias .maximum(5)\\nz.number().check(z.positive());        // alias .gt(0)\\nz.number().check(z.nonnegative()); \\nz.number().check(z.negative()); \\nz.number().check(z.nonpositive()); \\nz.number().check(z.multipleOf(5));     // alias .step(5)\\n```\\n\\nIf (for some reason) you want to validate `NaN` , use `z.nan()` .\\n\\n```\\nz.nan().parse(NaN);              // ✅\\nz.nan().parse(\\\"anything else\\\");  // ❌\\n```\\n\\n\\n\\nhttps://zod.dev/api#integers\\n\\n## [Integers](https://zod.dev/api?id=integers)\\n\\nTo validate integers:\\n\\n```\\nz.int();     // restricts to safe integer range\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\nz.int32();   // restrict to int32 range\\n```\\n\\n\\n\\nhttps://zod.dev/api#bigints\\n\\n## [BigInts](https://zod.dev/api?id=bigints)\\n\\nTo validate BigInts:\\n\\n```\\nz.bigint();\\n```\\n\\nZod includes a handful of bigint-specific validations.\\n\\nZod Zod Mini     \\n```\\nz.bigint().check(z.gt(5n));\\nz.bigint().check(z.gte(5n));           // alias `.minimum(5n)`\\nz.bigint().check(z.lt(5n));\\nz.bigint().check(z.lte(5n));           // alias `.maximum(5n)`\\nz.bigint().check(z.positive());        // alias `.gt(0n)` \\nz.bigint().check(z.nonnegative()); \\nz.bigint().check(z.negative()); \\nz.bigint().check(z.nonpositive()); \\nz.bigint().check(z.multipleOf(5n));    // alias `.step(5n)`\\n```\\n\\n\\n\\nhttps://zod.dev/api#booleans\\n\\n## [Booleans](https://zod.dev/api?id=booleans)\\n\\nTo validate boolean values:\\n\\n```\\nz.boolean().parse(true); // => true\\nz.boolean().parse(false); // => false\\n```\\n\\n\\n\\nhttps://zod.dev/api#dates\\n\\n## [Dates](https://zod.dev/api?id=dates)\\n\\nUse `z.date()` to validate `Date` instances.\\n\\n```\\nz.date().safeParse(new Date()); // success: true\\nz.date().safeParse(\\\"2022-01-12T06:15:00.000Z\\\"); // success: false\\n```\\n\\nTo customize the error message:\\n\\n```\\nz.date({\\n  error: issue => issue.input === undefined ? \\\"Required\\\" : \\\"Invalid date\\\"\\n});\\n```\\n\\nZod provides a handful of date-specific validations.\\n\\nZod Zod Mini     \\n```\\nz.date().check(z.minimum(new Date(\\\"1900-01-01\\\"), { error: \\\"Too old!\\\" }));\\nz.date().check(z.maximum(new Date(), { error: \\\"Too young!\\\" }));\\n```\\n\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"Defining schemas\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"documentation\",\"url\":\"https://zod.dev/api#template-literals\"},{\"type\":\"document\",\"source\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Title: validate a field when another field changes\\nCategory: Q&A\\nAuthor: @aprilmintacpineda\\nTimestamp: September 23, 2025\\nBody:\\nHi, I'm trying to implement a feature that disallows a user from selecting the same things on different fields. Now I want the validation to run on multiple fields (`A`, `B`). When `A` changes, I want `B` to also be validated. Example below:\\r\\n\\r\\n```ts\\r\\nschema.superRefine((values, ctx) => {\\r\\n  if (\\r\\n    values.A === values.B\\r\\n  ) {\\r\\n    ctx.addIssue({\\r\\n      code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,\\r\\n      message: 'Please select a different value',\\r\\n      path: ['A'],\\r\\n    });\\r\\n\\r\\n    ctx.addIssue({\\r\\n      code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,\\r\\n      message: 'Please select a different value',\\r\\n      path: ['B'],\\r\\n    });\\r\\n  }\\r\\n})\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nThis is what happens:\\r\\n\\r\\nWhen I set A to a value and then select the same value in B, the error appears in B. When I try to select a different value for A, the error in B still remains (and vice-versa).\\r\\n\\r\\nWhat I expect:\\r\\n\\r\\nWhen I set A to a value and then select the same value in B, the error appears in B. When I try to select a different value for A, the error in B should go away.\\r\\n\\r\\nNote:\\r\\n\\r\\nI use react-hook-form and zodResolver and I have tried the following options but it didn't work:\\r\\n\\r\\n```ts\\r\\nmode: 'all',\\r\\nreValidateMode: 'onChange',\\r\\ncriteriaMode: 'all',\\r\\n```\\nComments:\\n\\n    Comment #1 from @dosubot on September 23, 2025:\\n\\n    <!-- Answer -->\\nI'm a bot trained on Zod's codebase & issues.\\n\\nZod recalculates all validation errors from scratch every time you call `.parse` or `.safeParse` on your schema—errors don't persist unless the data still violates your validation rules. Your `.superRefine()` logic is correct for cross-field validation: if `A === B`, issues are added to both fields. If you change either field so they are no longer equal, Zod will not include those errors in the next validation result.\\n\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\nIf you're seeing errors remain after changing a field, it's almost certainly due to how your form library (like React Hook Form or Formik) manages validation and error state. Many form libraries only re-validate the field that changed, not the whole form, so errors for related fields may not be cleared automatically. To fix this, ensure your form triggers validation for the entire object/schema whenever either field changes. This way, Zod will re-validate both fields and clear any errors that no longer apply.\\n\\nFor more on how Zod handles errors and cross-field validation, see the docs on [`superRefine`](https://zod.dev/api?id=superrefine) and [error formatting](https://zod.dev/error-formatting). Also, note that in Zod 4, `.superRefine()` is deprecated in favor of `.check()`, but the cross-field validation logic is the same ([PR #4954](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/pull/4954)).\\n\\nIf this solves your issue, feel free to close the thread! If you need integration tips for a specific form library, let me know which one you're using.\\n\\n\\n<!-- Dosu Comment Footer -->\\n*To reply, just mention [@dosu](https://go.dosu.dev/dosubot).*\\n\\n---\"},{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"\\nReact Hook Form only re-validates the field that changed by default, so cross-field errors (like your A/B scenario) may not clear automatically. To fix this, use a `useEffect` hook to watch changes on one field and trigger validation on the other. For example:\\n\\n```ts\\nuseEffect(() => {\\n  const sub = watch((_, { name }) => {\\n    if (name === 'A') trigger('B');\\n    if (name === 'B') trigger('A');\\n  });\\n  return () => sub.unsubscribe();\\n}, [watch, trigger]);\\n```\\n\\nThis ensures that when either `A` or `B` changes, both fields are re-validated and errors are cleared as expected. This approach is recommended in the React Hook Form + Zod community—see [this GitHub issue for more details](https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4847).\\n\\nThis works with both Zod 3 and Zod 4. If you want advice on `.superRefine` vs `.check`, let me know your Zod version. If this solves your issue, feel free to close the thread!\\n\\n\\n<!-- Dosu Comment Footer -->\\n*To reply, just mention [@dosu](https://go.dosu.dev/dosubot).*\\n\\n---\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"validate a field when another field changes\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"github_discussion\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/discussions/5276\"},{\"type\":\"document\",\"source\":{\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Github Issue by @Asteroid77 created on March 21, 2025. The current state of the issue is: closed\\n\\nI have a schema as shown below, and now I can't get the error of field \\\"name\\\" before other fields validation completes until the validation of the field \\\"name\\\" rest only.\\nAny idea for my situation? I've searched through existing issues trying to find a solution but haven't identified a concrete approach. This likely indicates fundamental misunderstandings in my Zod implementation patterns. Would anyone be willing to audit my usage? Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.\\nThanks!\\n```\\nconst $t = getI18NInstance()\\nexport const stepOneSchema = z\\n  .object<ZodSchemaBuilder<Omit<RegisterRequest, 'code' | 'bizId' | 'name'> & { name?: string }>>({\\n    registerType: requiredSchema($t('account.register.type.text')),\\n    phone: requiredSchema($t('account.phone.text')).pipe(phoneReExpSchema()),\\n    password: requiredSchema($t('account.password.new')),\\n    confirmPassword: requiredSchema($t('account.password.confirm.normal.text')),\\n    name: z.string().optional(),\\n  })\\n  .refine((data) => data.password === data.confirmPassword, {\\n    message: $t('account.password.confirm.error.text'),\\n    path: ['confirmPassword'],\\n  })\\n  .superRefine((data, ctx) => {\\n    if (!data.name) {\\n      ctx.addIssue({\\n        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,\\n        message: `${userNameLabelText(data.registerType)}${$t('common.notEmpty')}`,\\n        path: ['name'],\\n      })\\n    }\\n  })\\n```\\n---\\nComment #1 by @greenheadHQ:\\n\\n@Asteroid77 Thanks! The example code you provided was a great help in solving my problem.\\n\\n\"}],\"type\":\"content\"},\"title\":\"The error message of a required field depends on another field, but superRefine cannot run before other fields' validation completes.\",\"context\":null,\"record_type\":\"github_issue\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/colinhacks/zod/issues/4043\"}]}"
